subject: LU-7447 lfsck: correct nlink attr for new created dir

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/35779/

Patch Set 1:

I think it'd be easier to reset nlink in osd-ldiskfs/ and do not introduce additional technical debt.

Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

PPC64 Builder: build succesful

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 
 
https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/35779/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1:

> I think it'd be easier to reset nlink in osd-ldiskfs/ and do not introduce additional technical debt.

What the technical debt means? Save the nlink attr before ldiskfs_add_dot_dotdot(), and restore it after ldiskfs_add_dot_dotdot(), right?

In fact, the patch will set guard in the osd-ldiskfs/osd-zfs, with such guard/check, it guarantees that creating directory must follow the rule of add_ref() called before inserting dot/dotdot. I think there will be not technical debt in future.

Patch Set 1:

the debt is that this the patch introduces more changes relying on wrong implementation instead of fixing the original problem.
OSD API doesn't really care about nlink and about order of nlink++/insert.
it's a problem of ldiskfs OSD to implement the API properly.

Patch Set 1:

> the debt is that this the patch introduces more changes relying on wrong implementation instead of fixing the original problem. OSD API doesn't really care about nlink and about order of nlink++/insert. it's a problem of ldiskfs OSD to implement the API properly.

But we have done that already, for example, it is required to insert dot entry before inserting dotdot entry; otherwise the caller will get -EINVAL. As the point, it should be technical debts also..

Patch Set 1:

yes, I agree that's a debt as well. but this isn't an excuse to add more and more? probably it's a god time to think how to fix both?

Patch Set 1:

Then how about the solution of  saving the nlink attr before ldiskfs_add_dot_dotdot(), and restore it after ldiskfs_add_dot_dotdot()?

Patch Set 1:

very appreciated if you can try this.

parents.subject: LU-7371 test: wrong read length over isize

commit.subject: LU-7447 lfsck: correct nlink attr for new created dir
commit.message:
LU-7447 lfsck: correct nlink attr for new created dir

For new created directory object, there are three main operations:
1) insert dot entry
2) insert dotdot entry
3) ref_add on the directory object.

Sometimes, the 3rd step maybe ahead of 1st, sometimes maybe between
1st and 2nd. Usually, the developers would think that such order is
not important. But in fact, such assumption isn't true, because the
ldiskfs will set the new created directory object's nlink attr as 2
when inserting dotdot entry. Then if ref_add() is called after ".."
inserted, the empty directory object's nlink attr is 3.

To avoid above trouble and make the OSD API to be order-independent,
we will make the osd-ldiskfs to backup the new created dir object's
nlink attr before calling ldiskfs_add_dot_dotdot(), then restore it
after ldiskfs_add_dot_dotdot() called.

On the other hand, this patch also adds some missed logic for lfsck
declaring to insert dot/dotdot entries, remove some redundant logic.

Add e2fsck check for on-disk consistency verification after LFSCK.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I065f683388417f3e23d47471c065419bd9bfcb19
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/17269
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
